summary.intent: Split large Rust files (over 1,500 lines) in the allowlist of scripts/check-file-size.sh into idiomatic Rust modules by domain, then verify no behavioral regressions and clean up files approaching the 1,500-line limit.
summary.outcome: ~160 new module files created, all files brought under 1,500 lines with zero allowlisted entries, clippy clean, 4,633 tests passing. Two bugs found and fixed (undeleted runner.rs, merge conflict in cron_tool.rs). Six additional files near the limit were split in parallel subagents before hitting the rate limit.

repo: scripts/check-file-size.sh enforces a 1,500-line limit per Rust file with an explicit allowlist; clearing the allowlist without fixing the files causes false passes.
The workspace uses a nightly Rust toolchain; local-llm features require CUDA/CMake and cannot be built on macOS without those tools.
Behavioral changes from two main merges (PR #696 codex/fix-tool-regressions, PR #499 feat/voice-base-url) were interleaved with the refactoring commits, making pure-move verification non-trivial.
78 Rust files remain over 1,000 lines after the refactoring; 6 were within 50 lines of the 1,500-line gate.

code.finding: Original runner.rs (9,414 lines) was never deleted after runner/mod.rs + submodules were created, causing a Rust module conflict. Fix: delete runner.rs.

Unresolved merge conflict markers (<<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>>) left from merging main into the branch. The HEAD side (mod tests;) was correct since cron_tool/tests.rs already contained all 25 test functions.

workflow: Running clippy on a cached build can give false clean results; always do a fresh check (cargo clean or per-crate check) after structural refactors.
Splitting large files in parallel subagents per crate is effective since crates are independent — no file conflicts between agents.
When verifying a pure-move refactor, diffing function counts (grep -c 'fn ') across old and new locations is a fast sanity check before deeper inspection.
Merging main mid-refactor intermingles behavioral commits with structural ones, requiring explicit commit-to-PR mapping to separate intentional changes from moved code.

friction: Rate limit hit twice during parallel subagent splits, interrupting work mid-batch and requiring the user to finish manually or wait for reset.
Clippy's cached results masked the runner.rs / runner/mod.rs module conflict until a clean per-crate build was attempted.
Merge conflict in cron_tool.rs (from merging main during the refactor) left unresolved markers that inflated the file to 2,090 lines, causing a false file-size failure.
CUDA/CMake build errors on macOS prevented running the full test suite including local-llm features, requiring feature-flag exclusion workarounds.
Distinguishing pure structural moves from behavioral changes required multi-step git archaeology (merge-base, first-parent log, commit-to-PR mapping).
open_items: 72 remaining Rust files between 1,000–1,450 lines that could trip the 1,500-line gate with future feature additions.

The 6 files split in the final batch (openai/provider.rs, httpd/server.rs, telegram/outbound.rs, provider-setup implementation, channel_events/commands.rs, web/terminal.rs) need clippy and test verification — session ended at rate limit before confirmation.
graphql/tests/integration.rs at 1,469 lines was in the top-10 list but not included in the final split batch.
Local-llm / CUDA crates are untested on this machine; their build and test status after the refactor is unverified.
